resources.string: Keep your files private with VeraCrypt-compatible encrypted containers.
Your data is protected with AES-256, Twofish, and Serpent — the same algorithms trusted by security professionals worldwide.
To anyone watching, Arcanum looks like a regular calculator app. Only your secret PIN unlocks it.
View your photos and videos privately inside encrypted containers, without leaving any traces.
Enter your panic PIN to instantly wipe all traces of Arcanum from your device.
Shows progress while creating an encrypted vault
Backup stopped because the app was moved to background
Unmount the container before backing it up
Configure and save backup settings first
Shows progress while changing a vault password
Shows progress while expanding a volume
File is too small or not sector-aligned
Check the file name and available vault space
Choose another folder or try again.
Temporary decrypted copies were not created.
Use Decrypt to save folders with their structure.
Imported successfully, but Android did not allow deleting the source.
Exported successfully, but some vault files could not be deleted.
Import stopped — files would overwrite the hidden volume
Not enough space in the vault
Use 4-128 characters. Digits, letters and symbols are allowed.
Tap the keyboard icon for a 4-128 character password.
Your app password is set. Enter it on the lock screen to access Arcanum.
4-12 digits, or tap the keyboard icon for a 4-128 character password
This vault doesn\'t contain any photos or videos
⚠ This will update the EXIF data inside the file and move it in the timeline.
No EXIF data available for this file.
Tap + to import files or create a folder
To open this file, a temporary decrypted copy will be created. It will be deleted automatically when you return to Arcanum.
Files will be decrypted to the selected folder. Default target: %1$s. Folder structure is always preserved.
Your changes have not been saved.
Could not load this text file.
This text file is too large to edit here.
Create or open an existing VeraCrypt container
The vault will be locked until you enter the password again.
The container could not be found at its saved path. It may have been moved or renamed by another app.
The container will be removed from the list. The file will not be deleted.
The vault will be removed from the list. The container file on disk will not be deleted.
The container file and all encrypted data inside it will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
You will need to enter your PIN to unlock again.
Unmount when the device screen locks
Unmount when the app is sent to background
Move vault to a shared storage folder
Please unmount the container before renaming it
Pin vaults with biometric unlock to the top
Prevents outer volume writes from reaching the hidden area
The outer volume is mounted. The hidden volume is protected from accidental overwriting.
One or more keyfiles not accessible
The keyfiles saved for this container could not be read — they may have been moved or deleted. Mount manually to select them again.
You\'ll need to enter your password manually to unlock \"%1$s\".
Lock when app goes to background
Allow vaults to use biometric unlock when it is enabled for that vault.
After every wrong PIN attempt, silently capture one front-camera photo when Android allows it.
Blocks screenshots and screen recording. Disabling exposes vault contents to any app that requests screen capture — strongly recommended to keep enabled.

Without screen protection, any app with the "appear on top" permission — or a screen recorder — can capture what\'s shown in Arcanum, including passwords and private files.\n\nOnly disable this if you fully trust your environment.
Remove this task from Android\'s recent apps screen when possible.
Change the launcher name, icon, and first screen.
Choose a disguise profile, then press Apply. The launcher will not change until you apply it.
Hold the fake screen title for about 7 seconds to open PIN entry.
Custom profile opens the standard PIN or biometric screen.
The app will appear as %1$s. To enter Arcanum, open it, type your PIN on the calculator keypad, then hold "=" for about 7 seconds.
The app will appear as %1$s. To enter Arcanum, open it and hold the app title at the top of the fake screen for about 7 seconds.
The custom profile lets you set your own launcher name, icon, and background color. Opening it goes directly to the standard PIN or biometric screen.
This choice is only previewed now. It will not be saved until you press Apply.
Set any visible app name, choose an icon from the library, and pick the background color used behind the icon.

Arcanum can disguise itself as another app on your home screen. The default disguise is Calculator.

To unlock from the calculator, enter your PIN using the number buttons, then hold "=" for about 7 seconds.

You can change the disguise profile or disable it later in Settings.
System is up to date. No updates found.
Live CPU, memory, and service load
Optimization complete. Background tasks were closed.
Countdown timer with custom duration and alert
Precision stopwatch with laps and session history
Battery, storage, network, and update checks
Primary inbox, sync status, and notifications
Camera LED and screen light controls
Daily utilities, conversion tools, and diagnostics
Cache, storage, network, and service maintenance
Memory, CPU, and background task overview
Entering Panic PIN silently wipes selected data
Tap to set up a Panic PIN
Delete all vaults and reset app to factory state
Remove biometric access from all vaults
Wiped data cannot be recovered. Test with a decoy vault first.
Your Panic PIN and all wipe settings will be permanently deleted.
Delete source files after they are imported into the vault, when Android allows it.
Delete vault files only after they are exported successfully.
The container is mounted. It will be unmounted automatically before backup.
Only after the new backup uploads successfully
Confirm your identity to view or change saved credentials.
%1$s • the container file will be copied as is
Regular MEGA account: sign in with email and password, then upload the backup to the selected folder.
Check and save the new settings first
Could not open system identity check
Set up a screen lock on the device first
Confirm your identity to change saved credentials
Could not create a test file in the selected folder
No write access to the selected folder
Could not create the backup file
Could not finish writing the backup
Backup was written, but the file was not found
The selected folder is no longer available
New backup was created, but the old one could not be deleted
New backup was created, but the old one could not be deleted: %1$s
New backup was created, but the old one could not be deleted: different S3 location
New backup was created, but the old location does not look like MEGA
S3 settings did not pass validation: %1$s
S3 returned an invalid uploaded object size
MEGA settings did not pass validation: %1$s
MEGA did not return the account salt
MEGA returned a new account key format that cannot be opened through the legacy email/password API. Sign in to this account once through MEGAcmd or MEGAsync, then try again in Arcanum.
MEGA did not return the account key. Check the email and password.
MEGA did not return the account private key
MEGA did not return a session id
MEGA did not return an upload completion handle
MEGA did not return the account root folder
MEGA created the folder but did not return a handle
MEGA did not return an upload URL
MEGA uploaded the file but did not return a handle
MEGA returned 402 but did not include an X-Hashcash challenge. Headers: %1$s
MEGA did not accept Hashcash after %1$d attempts. Try later or switch networks.
MEGA API did not respond after retries
Could not read the backup file for MEGA
MEGA returned a damaged RSA key
MEGA returned an incomplete RSA key
MEGA returned a short session id
MEGA Hashcash was not solved in %1$d sec.
MEGA is temporarily unavailable, try again later
MEGA is busy, try again later
MEGA: file or folder already exists
MEGA could not find the account, folder, or file. Check email/password and path.
MEGA blocked access to the account
MEGA: no access to the selected folder
MEGA requires a two-factor code or rejected password-only login
Pure black backgrounds with frosted glass
Dynamic colors require Android 12 or higher.
VeraCrypt-compatible encrypted vault manager for Android
See what changed in this version
Password fields now show suggestions from your autofill service (e.g. KeePassDX). Selecting a credential automatically fills the Confirm Password field too.
Keyfiles are now read directly into memory and never written to the app\'s cache. Previous builds left a temporary plaintext copy on disk during mounting.
Containers protected with a keyfile can now be unlocked with biometrics. If a keyfile is later moved or deleted, a clear error screen guides you back to manual mounting.
Manually mounting a container with the biometric toggle on now refreshes the saved credentials, which is useful when changing your password or keyfiles.
When mounting an outer volume with hidden volume protection enabled, a confirmation screen now appears to confirm that protection is active.
Creating containers on arm32 (armeabi-v7a) devices produced a broken, unreadable container. The XTS encryption layer silently operated as 32-bit due to a type definition issue, corrupting the header.
Enable in debug settings to watch every cipher and PRF combination tried in real time as your container is being unlocked.
A banner appears the first time you open the app after an update. Tap it to open the What\'s New screen.
Rename your containers directly from the container config sheet.
Tab switches are now animated, and password fields no longer show IME suggestions for better privacy.

• Containers with a custom PIM now open correctly because the iteration formula matches the VeraCrypt spec.
• Keyboard and text cursor now close when tapping the mount button.
• Swipe-dismissing the update banner no longer permanently marks it as seen.
• Biometric prompt now reappears correctly after the app auto-locks in the background.
Open containers created on Windows, macOS, or Linux with no conversion needed. AES, Twofish, Serpent and all cascades are supported.
BLAKE2s-256, SHA-512, and Whirlpool are fully compatible with VeraCrypt 1.26+.
Two passwords, two independent datasets. Plausible deniability under coercion is mathematically impossible to disprove.
Add one or more keyfiles as a second authentication factor required alongside the password to open the vault.
Personal Iterations Multiplier for fine-tuned key derivation strength and unlock time.
Duress PIN silently wipes vaults, settings, and biometrics with timing indistinguishable from a normal unlock.
Arcanum looks like a regular calculator app. Your secret PIN is the only way in.
Save vault credentials to the hardware Keystore and unlock with fingerprint.
Browse photos and videos directly inside mounted vaults without extracting them.
Import, export, copy, move, and manage files inside encrypted vaults.
Stream audio from encrypted vaults without writing decrypted copies to disk.
Pure-black backgrounds with frosted-glass blur throughout the app.
Show a live terminal log during container mounting. Resets when debug mode is disabled.
Debug mode exposes cryptographic handles, keystore type, container paths and other sensitive data. Your device has no screen lock — anyone with physical access can enable this mode.
Panic Mode is now active. Enter the Panic PIN in the calculator to trigger a silent wipe.
Do not confuse your Panic PIN with your main PIN.\n\nEntering the Panic PIN will immediately begin wiping your configured data — this cannot be undone.
4–12 digits. Must differ from your main PIN.
The incomplete file will be deleted.
A regular encrypted vault protected by a single password.
Two passwords, two sets of data. Plausible deniability under coercion.
Private storage inside the app. No extra permissions needed.
Allow Android to back up this container to the cloud
Save to a user-accessible folder on your device storage.
Grant \"All files access\" so the app can create vaults outside its private folder.
A vault with this file name already exists in app storage.
All algorithms are fully compatible with desktop VeraCrypt
Use a strong password. If you forget it, your data cannot be recovered.
This password unlocks the decoy outer volume. You will set a separate hidden volume password later.
This is the outer (decoy) volume password. Anyone who gets access to it will see the outer volume, not the hidden one.
Keep these files safe — they are required to open the vault
A keyfile adds a second factor of authentication. Both the password and all keyfiles are required to open the vault.
Below default — use a strong password
PIM below 485 requires a password of at least 20 characters
PIM below 485 requires a password of at least 20 characters
There is no way to recover your password. Write it down and keep it safe.
~%1$s for any size · Less secure — patterns may be detectable on advanced forensics. Recommended for most users.
~%1$s · Fills entire volume with random data. Maximum security, no data patterns. Recommended for sensitive data.
Choose how files are organized inside the vault
Random movement increases the cryptographic strength of the keys
Move your finger randomly until the bar is full (%1$d%%)
Your outer volume has been created. Now set up the hidden volume inside it.
A hidden volume lives inside the outer volume. When you enter the outer password, only the outer data is visible. Enter the hidden password to reveal the hidden data.

• Use a different password for the hidden volume than the outer volume.\n• The hidden volume occupies the end of the outer volume\'s data area.\n• Writing too many large files to the outer volume may overwrite the hidden volume.
Choose the cipher for the hidden volume
Hidden volume must be between %1$d MB and %2$d MB (outer: %3$d MB, 4 MB reserved for outer data)
Must be different from your outer volume password
If the hidden and outer passwords are the same, plausible deniability is compromised.
PIM controls PBKDF2 iterations. Leave blank for default.
Random movement increases the cryptographic strength of the hidden volume keys
The hidden volume has been successfully created. If all instructions were followed, it should be impossible to prove that the hidden volume exists, even when the outer volume is mounted.
WARNING: If you do not protect the hidden volume when mounting the outer volume, do not write to the outer volume — you may overwrite and damage the hidden volume!
To protect the hidden volume, enable "Protect hidden volume" and enter the hidden volume password when mounting the outer volume.
This may take a moment.\nPlease don\'t close the app.
Almost there — some vaults take a bit longer.
Deriving decryption keys — this is intentionally slow.
Nearly done — brute-force protection takes time.
This may be due to one or more of the following:
Volume uses an old algorithm that has been removed.
Make sure all files are saved before unmounting.
Increase the size of this volume
Save a backup of the volume header
Update the keyfile for this volume
Usable size excludes the VeraCrypt header and embedded backup header.
Creates a larger temporary volume, copies data, verifies it, then replaces the original.
Uses native preflight and runs only when the filesystem can be expanded safely.
Unmount the vault before expanding it.
Hidden volume credentials are required to preserve the hidden volume.
In-place expansion is disabled for hidden volumes.
Wrong password, PIM, PRF, or keyfile.
Not enough space, or the target size is not larger than the current size.
Not enough free space. Required with safety reserve: %1$s. Available: %2$s.
Cannot verify free space for this storage provider. Move the vault to app storage or internal storage, then expand it.
In-place expansion is unavailable for this volume; use safe rebuild.
Cannot read or write the volume file.
This storage provider does not support replacing the volume in place.
Could not replace the original volume.
Not enough free space. Free up at least %1$s and try again.
Files are stored in app-private storage, invisible to other apps and file managers.
Only Arcanum can read, move, or delete them.
Deleted when you uninstall the app (unless backed up).
Files are moved to shared internal storage, accessible via file managers.
Easier to copy files to a PC or share between devices.
The operation may take several minutes for large containers.
Keep the app in the foreground and do not lock the screen.
When enabled, Android may back up the vault to your Google account. Disable if you prefer your vault to never leave the device.
Default: Arcanum folder in app storage
Please keep the app open and do not lock your screen.
The vault file has been moved and the database record updated.
Too many attempts. Try again in %d seconds
Show a fake calculator on launch. Your PIN is the only way in.
To unlock Arcanum, open the calculator and enter your PIN using the number buttons. Then hold "=" for about 7 seconds.
Here\'s what arrived in this release
Enter the current password and keyfiles for this container.
Enter the new password, PIM and keyfiles. You can also change the PKCS-5 PRF.
Choose how many times the old header is overwritten before writing the new one.
The container password has been updated successfully.
Fastest. Overwrites the old header once with random data.
Recommended. Three random overwrites per the DoD standard.
Extended DoD standard with seven overwrite passes.
Paranoia-level. Thirty-five passes following Gutmann\'s method.
Password change is not complete. Exit anyway?
This container must be unmounted before changing the password.
Re-encrypt header with a different keyfile set
Enter the password used to unlock this container
Add keyfiles for the container, or disable to remove all keyfiles
Keyfiles disabled — container will be unlocked by password alone
Add Keyfiles is disabled. If this container currently uses keyfiles, they will all be removed. The container will be accessible by password alone.
Move your finger across the canvas to collect entropy for the new header salt
Keyfile change is not complete. Exit anyway?
Keyfiles updated. Use the new keyfiles to unlock this container.
Shows progress while changing vault keyfiles
Save a backup of the volume header to an external file. Use it to restore access if the header becomes damaged.
Store the backup file somewhere safe. Use Restore Header to recover the volume if the header is ever damaged.
This will overwrite the primary volume header. Make sure you enter the correct password.
The primary volume header has been restored. You can now access the volume with the original credentials.
Restore from an embedded or external backup
Restore the header from a backup copy
Encrypt and lock this vault now
Fingerprint or face unlock is enabled for this vault.
Enable it on the next manual unlock by entering the vault password.
Saved for this vault, but app-wide biometric unlock is off.
Enable fingerprint or face unlock in app security settings first.
Create a manual backup of the encrypted container file
The vault will be unmounted automatically before backup
The vault will be unmounted automatically before this action
%1$d source file(s) were imported before the error. Delete those original files now?
Timer finished. Stop the alarm to dismiss it.
Enter a name before applying this custom profile.
